title: LangChain broad load() allowlists enabled attacker-controlled object revival, chat-history poisoning, and possible credential or request side effects
impact_surface: serialized constructor payloads; run inputs and outputs; message history; deserialization trust boundary
mitre_atlas_mapping: no_direct_case_study_match_found; cautious technique context only: attacker-controlled context or state injection into runtime memory surfaces
owasp_llm_agentic_mapping: OWASP LLM01 Prompt Injection; OWASP LLM06 Sensitive Information Disclosure; OWASP LLM08 Excessive Agency; OWASP agentic: memory poisoning; OWASP agentic: exfiltration
mitigation_or_response: Public CVE text states LangChain fixed this unsafe deserialization issue in versions 0.3.85 and 1.3.3.; The vendor GitHub advisory also deprecates older runtime surfaces such as RunnableWithMessageHistory, astream_log(), and astream_events(version="v1") and says load() and loads() should be used only with trusted LangChain manifests or trusted storage.
